title: NIS2 Board-Level Cyber Risk Reporting Distribution Audit
exclusions: Does not read mailbox bodies, does not prove directors opened attachments, and does not decide whether a postponed report legally satisfies national transposition of NIS2 — counsel remains accountable for those judgments.
evidence: Weekly `UC-22.2.42` JSON/CSV export with `evidence_id`, underlying `_audit` query scoped to `savedsearch_name`, and the signed timestamp metadata referenced in `evidenceSigning` for chain-of-custody.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.20 requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.20 (Governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.42: NIS2 Board-Level Cyber Risk Reporting Distribution Audit.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.42' running on index _audit and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture: lookup row report_name="NIS2_Board_Risk_Monthly" quarterly frequency; `_audit` contains no `action=search` events whose `savedsearch_name` equals that string in the last two quarters — weekly run returns `missed_cadence=1`, populates `evidence_id`, and lists the stale `distribution_list` for remediation.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fixture sanity check geared toward non-alerts: `_audit` includes a fresh `action=search` hit 18 days ago for the same saved search with `info` mentioning `sendemail`, while the lookup still expects `monthly` cadence — `days_since` stays 18, below the **35-day** monthly tolerance, so the outer `where` clause removes the row and the alert remains silent which auditors treat as the desired healthy state.

dataSources: `index=_audit` with `sourcetype=audittrail` (Splunk internal audit trail for scheduled searches) carrying `action`, `info`, `search_id`, `user`, and `savedsearch_name` when the scheduler renders PDFs and invokes `sendemail`. Expected `info` strings include renderer hints and mail-transport markers. Coverage expectations and board distribution lists are versioned in `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` (`report_name`, `frequency`, `distribution_list`, `last_expected_date`). Platform context: Splunk Cloud always retains `_audit`; Splunk Enterprise installs should mirror this visibility with admin roles — see [Splunkbase 361](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/361) packaging for on-prem Enterprise.
app: Splunk Enterprise 9.x ([Splunkbase 361](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/361)) or Splunk Cloud Platform — no extra TA required because `_audit` ships with the deployment.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_board_report_schedule.csv
| eval report_key=lower(report_name)
| join type=left report_key [
    search index=_audit sourcetype=audittrail action=search earliest=-180d
    | where match(info,"(?i)sendemail|pdf|rendered")
    | eval report_key=lower(savedsearch_name)
    | stats latest(_time) as last_delivery by report_key
]
| eval expected_cadence_days=case(frequency="monthly",35,frequency="quarterly",100,frequency="weekly",10,1=1,14)
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(last_delivery), round((now()-last_delivery)/86400,0), null())
| eval missed_cadence=if(isnull(last_delivery),1,if(days_since>expected_cadence_days,1,0))
| where missed_cadence=1
| eval evidence_id=md5(report_name . "|" . frequency . "|" . if(isnotnull(last_delivery), tostring(last_delivery), "no_audit_hit"))
| table report_name, frequency, distribution_list, last_expected_date, last_delivery, days_since, expected_cadence_days, evidence_id
description: Reconciles each `report_name` from `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` against `index=_audit` scheduler events and highlights board cyber packs whose PDF/email dispatch is absent or older than the policy cadence encoded in `frequency`.
value: Gives the board secretary and incident leadership an automatic paper trail that scheduled cyber risk briefings actually left Splunk on time, which matters when regulators ask whether oversight artefacts reached directors—not merely whether dashboards exist.
implementation: (1) Keep `savedsearch_name` identical to `report_name` (case-insensitive) or maintain a mapping lookup. (2) Ensure alert actions log `sendemail` with `format=pdf` where possible so `info` matches the SPL predicate. (3) Delegate ownership to Head of IR with secretariat co-ownership documented in the runbook. (4) Weekly schedule with **180-day** lookback. (5) Archive positive outcomes to `index=audit_evidence` if your programme requires WORM storage beyond `_audit` retention.
visualization: Table of missed rows with `distribution_list`, `last_expected_date`, and `evidence_id`; timeline of `last_delivery`; single value count of breaches by `frequency`.

references.title: Splunk Docs — About auditing Splunk activity
Splunk Docs — Schedule reports in Splunk Web

knownFalsePositives:
• **Executive read-only SharePoint drop instead of email** — Distinguish: board portal ingestion skipped `sendemail` but files landed in Microsoft 365; `info` lacks mail tokens. Suppress: add `alternate_delivery=sharepoint` to `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` and `lookup` it to bypass email predicates when flagged.
• **Saved search renamed mid-quarter** — Distinguish: `_audit` shows a new `savedsearch_name` while the lookup still lists the legacy `report_name`. Suppress: maintain `nis2_report_name_alias.csv` and `lookup` before the join to normalise keys.
• **High-availability failover reran the same job twice** — Distinguish: duplicate `search_id` values within seconds with identical `user`. Suppress: keep `| stats latest(_time)` as written; optionally `dedup search_id` inside the subsearch.
• **Quarterly blackout where the CIO postponed the pack during M&A quiet period** — Distinguish: formal board communication logged in the exception register. Suppress: time-bound exception rows in `nis2_reporting_exceptions.csv` with `quiet_until` evaluated via `inputlookup` in a preliminary `eval`.
• **PDF renderer succeeded but email action disabled in `savedsearches.conf`** — Distinguish: `info` references `rendered_report` without `sendemail`. Suppress: extend the `where match(info,...)` clause or add a correlated `index=_internal` sourcetype=scheduler filter after Change Approval documents the configuration drift.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110, Exchange Online message trace)
Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, board meeting task tracking)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (REST API for scheduled report audit)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• This UC implements NIS2 Art.20 — governance. Art.20(1) requires management bodies to approve cybersecurity risk-management measures and oversee their implementation. Art.20(2) requires management body members to follow cybersecurity training. This UC specifically addresses the oversight obligation by monitoring whether cyber risk reports are produced and distributed to the management body (board of directors, executive committee) on a regular cadence.
• **What "board-level cyber risk reporting" means**: NIS2 places personal accountability on management body members for cybersecurity failures (Art.32(6) for essential entities). To fulfil their oversight obligation, board members must receive regular, understandable cyber risk reports. This UC monitors: (1) whether scheduled cyber risk reports are generated (e.g., monthly CISO report, quarterly risk dashboard), (2) whether the reports are distributed to all board members, and (3) whether board members acknowledge receipt.
• **Data sources**:
  - `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler` — Splunk's internal scheduler log. Used to verify that scheduled reports (PDF deliveries, email alerts) are executing on schedule. Key fields: `savedsearch_name`, `status`, `run_time`.
  - `index=email sourcetype=ms:o365:messageTrace` — Microsoft 365 message trace logs from the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services. Used to verify that the CISO's cyber risk report email was delivered to all board members. Key fields: `SenderAddress`, `RecipientAddress`, `Subject`, `Status` (Delivered, Failed, Quarantined).
  - `nis2_board_reporting_schedule.csv` — the reporting schedule. Columns: `report_id` (e.g., "CISO-MONTHLY"), `report_name` (e.g., "Monthly CISO Cyber Risk Report"), `cadence` (monthly, quarterly, annual), `splunk_savedsearch_name` (the Splunk saved search that generates the report — if applicable), `distribution_list` (comma-separated email addresses of board members), `owner` (the report author — typically CISO), `last_sent_date` (date of last distribution).
  - `nis2_board_members.csv` — the management body register. Columns: `name`, `role` (board_chair, board_member, ceo, cfo, ciso), `email`, `training_status` (links to UC-22.2.41).
• **Splunk scheduled PDF delivery**: Splunk can generate PDF reports on a schedule and email them. If the CISO report is a Splunk dashboard, configure scheduled PDF delivery: Settings > Saved Searches > Edit Schedule > Enable PDF delivery. The UC verifies this execution via `_internal`.
• **RBAC**: role `nis2_governance_analyst` with `srchIndexesAllowed = _internal, email, itsm, audit_evidence`. Board reporting data reveals the names and email addresses of board members — handle as confidential.
• **Index retention**: `index=email` ≥ 1 year. `index=audit_evidence` ≥ 7 years.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**Reporting schedule CSV:**
Place `nis2_board_reporting_schedule.csv` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-governance/lookups/`. Example:
```csv
report_id,report_name,cadence,splunk_savedsearch_name,distribution_list,owner,last_sent_date
CISO-MONTHLY,Monthly CISO Cyber Risk Report,monthly,NIS2 Monthly Risk Summary,ceo@example.com;cfo@example.com;board-chair@example.com,CISO,2026-03-01
RISK-QUARTERLY,Quarterly Board Risk Dashboard,quarterly,NIS2 Quarterly Board Pack,board-all@example.com,Head of Risk,2026-01-15
INCIDENT-AD-HOC,Major Incident Board Notification,ad_hoc,,ceo@example.com;board-chair@example.com,CISO,
```

**Board members CSV:**
```csv
name,role,email,training_status
Anna Nordström,board_chair,board-chair@example.com,current
Erik Hansen,board_member,erik.hansen@example.com,current
Maria Silva,ceo,ceo@example.com,current
Thomas Berg,cfo,cfo@example.com,overdue
```

**Microsoft 365 message trace:**
Install the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110). Configure the message trace input to pull email delivery events:
- API: Microsoft 365 Reporting API (messageTrace)
- Filter: `SenderAddress=ciso@example.com` (or the report distribution account)
- Interval: 3600
- Index: `email`

Verify: `index=email sourcetype=ms:o365:messageTrace SenderAddress=ciso@example.com earliest=-30d | stats count dc(RecipientAddress) as recipients values(Status) as delivery_statuses`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.42 NIS2 Board Cyber Risk Report Audit`.

Understanding this SPL — design decisions:

• **Two detection branches**:
  - **Branch 1 — Report generation verification**: reads the reporting schedule CSV and checks `_internal` scheduler data for the corresponding Splunk saved search. If the saved search hasn't run successfully in the expected cadence window (monthly = 35 days, quarterly = 100 days), the report is flagged as `report_not_generated`.
  - **Branch 2 — Distribution verification**: for each scheduled report that was generated, checks the email message trace to verify delivery to all board members in the distribution list. If any board member didn't receive the email (status != "Delivered"), the report is flagged as `incomplete_distribution`.

• **Cadence-to-days mapping**: `eval max_days=case(cadence="monthly",35, cadence="quarterly",100, cadence="annual",380, 1==1, 35)`. The buffer (5-15 days beyond the nominal cadence) accounts for minor scheduling delays. A monthly report sent on the 3rd instead of the 1st should not trigger an alert.

• **Distribution list parsing**: `eval recipient_list=split(distribution_list, ";")` converts the semicolon-separated list into a multivalue field. `mvexpand recipient_list` creates one row per expected recipient. Each recipient is then checked against the message trace.

• **Ad-hoc reports**: reports with `cadence=ad_hoc` (e.g., major incident notifications) are not scheduled — they are triggered by events. The UC only monitors scheduled reports. Ad-hoc reports are tracked separately via UC-22.2.48.

Schedule: cron `0 9 * * 1` (weekly, Monday at 09:00). Trigger: `Number of results > 0`.

Alert actions: (1) For `report_not_generated`: email to the report owner (typically CISO) — the board hasn't received its scheduled risk update. (2) For `incomplete_distribution`: email to the CISO and the executive assistant handling board communications — some board members may have missed the report. (3) Write to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:board_reporting:evidence`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **Report generation check**: verify the CISO's monthly report saved search ran successfully in the last 30 days: `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler savedsearch_name="NIS2 Monthly Risk Summary" status=success earliest=-35d | stats count latest(_time) as last_run`. If `count > 0`, the report was generated.

(b) **Distribution verification**: after the monthly report is sent, check message trace: `index=email sourcetype=ms:o365:messageTrace SenderAddress=ciso@example.com Subject="*Monthly CISO*" earliest=-7d | stats count by RecipientAddress Status`. Every board member should appear with `Status=Delivered`.

(c) **Missing recipient detection**: temporarily remove one board member from the distribution list in the email system (not the CSV). Send the report. Run the UC. Confirm the missing recipient is flagged.

(d) **Scheduler failure simulation**: temporarily disable the CISO report saved search. Wait 35+ days (or adjust the test to a shorter window). Run the UC. Confirm `report_not_generated` is detected.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Board Reporting Compliance`):
• **Row 1 — Reporting posture**: Single-value `Reports on schedule`. Single-value `Reports overdue` (red ≥ 1). Single-value `Board members receiving reports`. Single-value `Delivery failures` (red ≥ 1).
• **Row 2 — Reporting schedule**: table from the CSV with `report_name`, `cadence`, `last_sent_date`, `days_since_last`, `status`. Colour-code: green=on schedule, amber=approaching due, red=overdue.
• **Row 3 — Delivery audit trail**: for the most recent report, table of `RecipientAddress`, `Status`, `delivery_time`. Shows which board members received the report and when.

Runbook (owner: CISO / Executive Office):
1. Weekly: review the alert. For overdue reports: investigate. Common causes: the CISO was on leave and no delegate was assigned, the saved search failed due to a search head restart, the email was quarantined by the spam filter.
2. Monthly: after sending the CISO report, verify delivery via the dashboard. Request read receipts from board members for critical reports.
3. Quarterly: present the reporting compliance evidence to the audit committee. Include: reporting schedule adherence, delivery confirmation, and any gaps.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Email message trace not available.** Microsoft 365 message trace data is only available for 10 days via the standard API, and 90 days via the extended report. Fix: ensure the Splunk TA is configured to poll at least daily. For historical data beyond 90 days, rely on Splunk's indexed copy.

• **Board report sent from a distribution list, not CISO's personal email.** The UC filters by `SenderAddress=ciso@example.com`, but the report may be sent from `board-reports@example.com`. Fix: update the SPL filter to match the actual sender address. Add it to the reporting schedule CSV as a `sender_email` field.

• **PDF delivery via Splunk doesn't generate message trace.** Splunk's built-in PDF delivery uses its own SMTP configuration, which may not route through Microsoft 365. Fix: verify SMTP routing. If Splunk sends via an internal relay, the message trace may show the relay as the sender. Adjust the filter accordingly.

• **Board member email changed.** A board member's email changed due to a name change or domain migration. The old email is in the CSV but the new email is in use. Fix: update the CSV. Cross-reference with the HR system or Active Directory to ensure email addresses are current.

• **Report generated but not distributed.** The saved search ran successfully (visible in `_internal`) but the email action failed. Common cause: SMTP server rejection, attachment too large (> 10MB for some mail servers), or the email action is not configured on the saved search. Fix: check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd "action.email" status=failure` for email delivery errors.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a dated log of whether the big cyber summary that is meant for directors was actually sent on schedule, so nobody has to guess if the board saw the risk picture on time.
